facts.text: Yonkers was named after Adriaen van der Donck's 1646 land patent, the estate called Colen Donck.
The Otis Elevator Company, founded by Elisha Otis in 1867, built the first safety elevator factory on the Hudson near present‑day Vark Street.
In 1888, Scottish‑born John Reid opened Saint Andrew’s Golf Club, the first golf course in the United States.
Yonkers’ historic Philipse Manor Hall State Historic Site sits near the former Van der Donck mill and hosts a museum of the 18th‑century estate.
The city’s 2015 Yonkers Brewing Company opened in the former Otis Trolley Barn, a National Register‑listed rail station.
Yonkers’ 2007 documentary “Brick by Brick: A Civil Rights Story” chronicles the city’s history of racial discrimination and housing segregation.
